pattern: Dynamic programming over prefixes records whether each prefix of the string is segmentable, extending one dictionary word at a time.
hints: Ask whether the whole string is reachable by laying down dictionary words end to end starting at position zero.
If a prefix ending at index i is segmentable and a dictionary word matches starting at i, then the prefix ending after that word is also segmentable.
Let dp[i] mean the first i characters are segmentable; dp[0] is true, and dp[j] becomes true when some dp[i] is true and s[i:j] is in the dictionary set.

edge_inputs.description: single character with no matching word
overlapping prefixes require backtracking past a dead end
almost segmentable but one leftover char

stress.description: long repetitive string with short words so many split points must be tried
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    words = ['a', 'aa', 'aaa']
    n = max(1, min(size, 300))
    s = 'a' * n
    return [s, words]
stress.note: highly ambiguous segmentation maximises dp transitions

stress.description: random segmentable string assembled from a small dictionary
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    words = ['ab', 'cd', 'abc', 'd', 'e']
    n = max(1, min(size, 300))
    parts = []
    total = 0
    while True:
        w = words[rng.randrange(len(words))]
        if total + len(w) > n:
            break
        parts.append(w)
        total += len(w)
    if not parts:
        parts = ['e']
    return [''.join(parts), words]
